# Kasper Bergholt

## Sitemaps

- [XML Sitemap](https://bergholt.net/sitemap.xml): Contains all public & indexable URLs for this website.

## Posts

- [Light / The Artificiality of Art / Degas' Obsession](https://bergholt.net/art) - Explore the art of Edgar Degas through a tiny paint fragment from "Dancers Practicing in the Foyer", which is a part of Glyptoteket's "Degas' Obsession" exhibit.
- [The Nikon D3 in 2026 ~ Review, Photos, Thoughts](https://bergholt.net/nikon) - I'm very pleased with the Nikon D3, which rekindled my interest in the visual arts & photography. See Nikon D3 photos, read reviews and see D3 specifications.
- [Garden of the Royal Danish Library in Light Snowfall](https://bergholt.net/snow) - Garden of the Royal Danish Library (Det Kongelige Biblioteks Have / Bibliotekshaven) in Light Snowfall, shot in Winter 2023 a few days before Christmas.
- [Viktor Shklovsky: On Flax from 'On Freedom of Art', The Third Factory, 1926](https://bergholt.net/flax) - Viktor Shklovsky: On Flax (1926). Explore key excerpts from The Third Factory on the digital archive of artist and photographer Kasper Bergholt.
- ['Flora Excursoria Hafniensis'](https://bergholt.net/flora) - 'Flora Excursoria Hafniensis' takes its part of departure in a book published in 1838 by the Danish Botanist Salomon T. N. Drejer of the same title.
- [System Poetry: Gibson, Wordsworth, Shakespeare (3 x Williams)](https://bergholt.net/poetry) - « The archivist produces more archive, and that is why the archive is never closed. It opens out of the future. » — Jacques Derrida In 2011 I wrote a system poem. In 2024 I inscribed it into the Bitcoin blockchain. In 2025 I finished a photographic work, ICE | July | 1982, built around
- [About](https://bergholt.net/about) - Kasper Bergholt is a Danish ultra-contemporary artist exploring the edges and margins of post-photography, poetry and visual art.
- ["The Time Is Out of Joint" in Shakespeare's Hamlet](https://bergholt.net/time) - Why does Hamlet say "the time is out of joint"? A close reading of the line from Act 1, Scene 5: Meter, grammar, editions, and Derrida's Specters of Marx.
- [Glossary of a Triptych: Glossematics and Heteroglossia](https://bergholt.net/glossary) - Table of contents Sun: A Triptych for the Solar Eclipse Grammar and Semantics Core Structure: Nouns, Verbs, Adverbs Structure of the 2011 Grid From 12 to 432 to the Perfect 72 The Fourth Adverb Closed the Gap Singular Versus Plural Verbs in the 2011 Version 2011 Vocabulary The 2026 Vocabulary The Deictic Cell Anchoring Using
- [The Leben CS-600 ~ An Introduction to Leben Tube Amplifiers](https://bergholt.net/tubes) - This blogpost on the Leben CS-600 and the Leben CS-600X tube amplifiers started out as a thorough introduction to vintage tubes after buying a Leben CS-600.
- ['Objects for an Ideal Home'](https://bergholt.net/objects) - 'Objects for an Ideal Home' is a series of photographs spanning sea urchins, tube amplifiers, teapots and vintage cameras & lenses.
- [Sunbuck Kondo Audio Note KSL-M7 preamplifier "clone"/ Exploding Capacitors](https://bergholt.net/clone) - 5U3C tube for a Kondo Audio Note KSL-M77 preamplifier clone. The preamplifier clone is based on two ECC81/6201 driver tubes fed by a 5U3C tube family.
- [6CS7 Tubes: Data, Specifications & Usage](https://bergholt.net/6cs7) - Originally designed for use in televisions and radios, 6CS7 tubes are rarely used in audio products. The chief designer and owner of Leben Hi-Fi Stereo Company, Mr. Taku Hyodo, who is often referred to as one of the eight best tube amplifier designers in Japan, has a fondness for the 6CS7 type, presumably because of
- [The Leben RS30-EQ RIAA, 6X5GT & ECC81 Tubes](https://bergholt.net/6x5-tubes) - The Leben RS-30EQ (Leben's only RIAA so far) which both looks and sounds stunning is a great, great product. Read RS30EQ review & see full specifications here.
- [Pentax 77mm 1.8 Limited Lens in 2026](https://bergholt.net/pentax) - The Pentax 77mm f/1.8 lens, the brainchild of Japanese lens designer Mr. Jun Hirokawa, a designer who clearly marches to the beat of his own drum.
- [Yixing Clay Teapot & Broussonetia Papyrifera Wrapper](https://bergholt.net/tea) - Pu Erh Tea wrapper hand-made from fibre of the Paper Mulberry tree (Latin: Broussonetia Papyrifera) by Essence of tea produced.
- [Bill Evans: The Paris Concert Edition Two](https://bergholt.net/evans) - "Unfortunately, many of our best performances are out there in the universe someplace, and you still as professionals have to go in at ten o’clock on Wednesday and make a record and hope that every few records you might catch a really good day. And the rest of it is being professional and, certainly as
- [The Pentax K10D in 2026 ~ Review, Photos, Impressions](https://bergholt.net/10d) - The Pentax K10D was released in 2006 following a range of CCD DSLR's starting with the *ist D released in 2003. Click to read Pentax K10D review & see photos.
- [The Nikon D4 in 2026 ~ Photos & Thoughts](https://bergholt.net/nikon-d4) - In June 2023, I got a cheap Nikon D3 to document a series of relatively rare vacuum tubes (starting with the 6CS7 family) for the Swiss Radiomuseum and Wikipedia. The project reignited my interest in visual art, leading to a number of photo series—perhaps a few too many (see an overview here). In December, I treated
- [The Carl Zeiss 35mm f/2 distagon in 2026](https://bergholt.net/zeiss) - I got the Zeiss 35mm f/2 with ZK mount after having worked with a Carl Zeiss Milvus 50mm f/2 macro with a Nikon D3 for various projects.
- [6CJ3, 6DN3, 6CM3, 6CH3 and 6DW4B damper tubes](https://bergholt.net/6dn3) - Yoshi Hontani from Leben has kindly confirmed by mail that the following damper tubes can be used in both the CS-600 & CS-600X amplifiers: 6DN3, 6CJ3, 6CM3, 6CH3 and 6DW4B.
- [Pentax *ist DS ~  2025 Review ~ Photos ~ Impressions](https://bergholt.net/istd-2) - The Pentax *istD series marked a significant milestone for Japanese brand Pentax when it was introduced in the early 2000s. See photos & read impressions here.

## Pages

- [Bakhtin, Chronotopes, Time Glitches](https://bergholt.net/chronotopes) - "In the literary artistic chronotope, spatial and temporal indicators are fused into one carefully thought-out, concrete whole", Mikhail Bakhtin.
- [Sun: Triptychs for Solar Eclipses](https://bergholt.net/sun) - In August 2026, a total solar eclipse will cross continental Europe, first visible there since August 11th, 1999. Sun is a triptych consisting of a system poem, which resolves a grammatical fault in a 2011 precursor, and three short video sequences: Aperture, Augury, and Apparition. The earlier version, I'd argue, is semantically richer; this one
- [Sandflugtsmonumentet, Tibirke, Tisvilde](https://bergholt.net/sand) - On May 20th, when I was ten years old, I had tonsillitis. It was probably due to sheer excitement over the fact that my mother and father were getting married. On the way to the wedding at Tibirke Church, my mother and grandfather stopped at the avenue leading up to the Sandflugtsmonumentet (The Sand Drift
- [cookies & privacy ~ bergholt.net](https://bergholt.net/cookies-privacy) - bergholt.net only uses cookies for logged in users (of which there is only one, me). No cookies are set for visitors, and no data is stored or shared.
- [Open calls](https://bergholt.net/open-call) - 'Moons, Castles, Trees | AI Chronotopes' was an open call for artists exploring the intersections of Artificial Intelligence, image-making, and temporality within the framework of the 2025–26 edition of The Wrong Biennale, the world’s largest decentralized digital art event. Participation was free and open to all; selected works will be exhibited online from November 2025
